Synthetic hourly energy consumption with covariates

Source: R/data.R
milt_energy.Rd

Hourly electricity consumption (MWh) for a single site over 60 consecutive days (1 440 observations), together with temperature and a weekend indicator. Designed for covariate-aware forecasting examples.

Usage

milt_energy

Format

A tibble::tibble() with 1 440 rows and 4 columns:

datetime

POSIXct (UTC). Timestamp at the start of each hour.

consumption

numeric. Energy consumed during the hour (MWh, non-negative).

temperature

numeric. Ambient temperature in degrees Celsius.

is_weekend

integer. 1 on Saturday or Sunday, 0 otherwise.

Source

Synthetically generated by data-raw/prepare_datasets.R.

See also

milt_series(), milt_air, milt_retail, milt_add_covariates()

Other datasets: milt_air, milt_retail

Examples

# \donttest{
data(milt_energy)
s <- milt_series(milt_energy,
                 time_col   = "datetime",
                 value_cols = "consumption",
                 frequency  = "hourly")
s
#> # A MiltSeries: 1440 x 1 [hourly]
#> # Time range : 2023-01-01 — 2023-03-01 23:00:00
#> # Components : consumption
#> # Gaps       : none
#> # A tibble: 6 × 4
#>   datetime            consumption temperature is_weekend
#>   <dttm>                    <dbl>       <dbl>      <int>
#> 1 2023-01-01 00:00:00        46.9       10.5           1
#> 2 2023-01-01 01:00:00        50.3        5.52          1
#> 3 2023-01-01 02:00:00        48.6        7.10          1
#> 4 2023-01-01 03:00:00        48.0        8.81          1
#> 5 2023-01-01 04:00:00        48.6        9.65          1
#> 6 2023-01-01 05:00:00        54.4       11.6           1
#> # … with 1434 more rows
# }
